title: convolvulus coccineus var. hederifolius
intro: Convolvulus coccineus var. hederifolius, also known as the Red Trumpet Vine or Ivy-leafed Bindweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Convolvulus coccineus var. hederifolius include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Convolvulus coccineus var. hederifolius can be prepared as an infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds with a solvent.
